sentences.text: Hi, how are you?
Hi, Becky.
How's it going?
Good!
Nice to meet you.
Nice to meet you as well, nice Orioles hat.
It's so funny, so my uncle, so I live in New Hampshire.
So by default, everybody up here are Red Sox fans.
But one of my uncles is a die-hard Orioles fan, which is super random for up here.
So every year, he goes to the Red Sox-Orioles game when they play at Fenway.
Yep, I bet.
My dad's a big Yankees fan, because I grew up in Jersey, so he loves to come up here and go to Yankees games, because it's a lot cheaper than going to Yankee Stadium.
For sure.
Yeah, that's so funny.
How are things going?
Good!
So thank you for getting this scheduled.
I was on the road last week, and I thought I was going to be able to make the call, and I just completely flopped and missed it.
So, Melissa wants me to kind of take a look at this for two reasons.
One, I oversee business development, so we use CoStar a lot for that.
Side of things?

But then, … The properties aren't good at using it for market surveys, so she wanted me to see if this would be, like, a better solution for the properties from a, you know, looking at rents, making rent adjustments, that kind of stuff perspective.
For sure, yeah.
She actually also shared a couple, pain points that you guys have with CoStar, and I just want to see if they align with your perspective as well.
So she had said, just, it's not updated information, it's not easy to use, and then obviously the biggest thing is the cost with CoStar.
Is that… does that align with your thought process?
Are there other things that you want to.
I use CoStar probably more than anybody at our company.
I think it's just, like, user error.
Costar is easy to use, the data is updated, like, by the minute, because it's all fed from Apartments.
com, and then if it's not from Apartments.
com, which most people are on it, most properties are on that.
They figure out… they have, like, analysts that aggregate all that data.
So, that, I don't… find accurate.
What I will say is, obviously, it's expensive, and again, our properties just don't use it, but I think that that could end up being the case with ApartmentIQ, too, so I gotta kind of evaluate that.
For sure, yeah.

I think that you're gonna see just… ApartmentIQ is a lot more, intuitive, it's a lot more user-friendly, and I apologize, I actually was under the impression that CoStar's data is, like, 4 to 6 weeks out of date, just because when they're collecting it, they're using call centers and stuff like that, so… the Apartments.
com information might be fed directly from property websites, or just them feeding it, not from property websites, obviously, but from ILS data.
But we're actually finding, because of the cost, a lot of customers are not advertising on Apartments.
com, so you're missing out on some of that inventory.
Yeah, you're right.
It's definitely moving more in that direction, too.
Even we're looking to be less dependent on ILSs, so it's like, how long will that data be?
We'll see.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, for sure, yeah.
Okay, so… I don't know how much background she gave you on ApartmentIQ, but super high level.
It's a fully automated data analytics tool providing information on all of your competitors, so they don't have to be a customer of ours.
We only use publicly available data, so it's all coming from property websites, and we're tracking close to 40 million units nationwide every day.
And as I mentioned, they don't have to be a customer, so they… we can track really anybody out there, as long as they have a website.
There's 3 functions within ApartmentIQ that I think that you'll find helpful.
The first is geared towards your stabilized portfolio, so the day-to-day operations, that's gonna be where the automated market surveys live.
among a lot of other information.

The second section's called Research, and this allows you to spin up a market survey for any property in the country that you don't currently own or manage, but also for the development side of things.

And then the third section's called Explore, and this is going to give more of that high-level market, sub-market look down to specific neighborhoods, and we're including demographic data as well as new supply information as well.
Justin is gonna go through the tool.
As he's going through things, feel free to hop in with any questions, but yeah, we'll go from there.
That's good, thanks.
Yeah, for sure.
Nice to meet you, Becky.
And I think, along with CoStar, what's interesting is we had a customer, a large enterprise customer, and they were doing an audit between CoStar coverage and ApartmentIQ coverage.
And of the, call it 1,200 properties they wanted to track.
And their comp, so call it 5 properties or 7 properties for each comp set.
Our coverage was 99%, and CoStar's was about 87%.
So, I was pretty surprised with that.

I assumed CoStar to have everybody, but I think in some of those tertiary markets or, you know, outside of the major MSAs, I think they do struggle, so… Kind of like Jamie was saying, I think we'll be able to give you a better accuracy and better coverage.
Boom.
But, yeah, I'll take you to the tool.
So, like Jamie said, 3 different sections of ApartmentIQ.
The first one, my properties, this is going to be your market surveys.
So, the way this is set up is that each one of your properties gets their own individual property card, and then all of the data is based off of that property's specific comp set.
Did you guys set this up for us initially?
Yes.
Okay.
So essentially, you provide us your list of properties and your comps, and our team does all the heavy lifting to set this up.
So when you guys log in, you'll see all of your properties here.
Perfect.
Yeah, absolutely.
Yeah, and then what's nice, too, is we offer unlimited users, so we can set up whoever's relevant to a property, and there's different permission levels.
So if you only want a site team to see their property, or a regional to see their portfolio, or a client to see their portfolio, you know, we can customize that setup.
And what's nice in regards to the comp sets is that your team's in control of them.
So, you know, you want to give us your comps?
Great, we can set that up.
Our system can also make some suggestions or recommendations for you, and then in the application, you can actually always add or remove a property to your comp set anytime you'd like.
So, really easy to control and manage whoever is relevant to you.
There's no cap either, so if you're in a dense market, you want to track 10 properties compared to a more tertiary market where you only want to track 4 or 5, totally up to you.
But you click into a property, and once you're in here, the very top, this will be your main navigation, so each property does have 4 reports associated with it.
A few things before we get going.
So, again, you log into here, all of this data is updated as of this morning.
What's nice, though, is we do give you the ability to aggregate the data over whatever timeframe is most relevant to your conversation.
We also have both PDF and Excel exports that can be downloaded anytime you'd like.
They can also be automated to be delivered to an inbox, on whatever frequency or cadence you'd like as well.
What's great is in the actual application, we let you manipulate this data pretty much however you'd want.
So you can look at the data based on specific comps or even subsets of comps.
You can look at it based off of bedroom or bathroom combos, square footage ranges, rent price ranges.
We even have the ability to filter in or out renovated units, classic units, penthouses, affordable units.
Down here on the left, all of these metrics, the entire way down, will be sortable from high to low or low to high.
And then, lastly, in the top right with this edit comp button, this is where you'd actually go to edit your comps.

So if you click into here, on the right will give you a map view of the area, blue pin into your property, white pins are properties that have already been added to your comp set, black pins are properties that are in the air that you might want to consider adding as well.
It's as simple as clicking Add Comp, all of the data plus the historicals are instantly added to your comp set.
Over here on the left, this is just a list view, and the major call-out is that we are going to assign a comp score to every property in the vicinity of yours.
So, this score here, it's a correlation score, but essentially a reference point in terms of how strong our system thinks a comp is.
And just so you're aware, these, these scores, they're derived from 5 factors.
So, we look at things like distance or proximity to your property, we look at vintage, total units, and then we do some pricing and amenity matching as well.
So, a combination of those five factors spits out these scores.
It's very objection… it's very objective, I mean, or very directionally correct, but at the end of the day, it's your call, you know, you know your markets.
So… So that's some of the functionality within the tool.
The reporting, or the data now, and we'll start with the market survey, but broken into five sections.
The first one here's property summary, so this is your high-level property data.
So, how far is each comp from you, who manages the property?
Give you a link to the Google reviews.
We can let you know if there are rent specials and concessions tied to the property, or specific floor plans or units in the property.
We're gonna show you the net effect… I'm sorry, the gross and net effect of rents at the property level as well.
And then what I like is we can actually present concessions two different ways.
One is a percentage of rent, or two as a value.
So, I know the marketing folks like percentages, the revenue groups, you know, they like value.
So, completely up to you how you display the information.
Section 2 is going to be your performance metrics, so your occupancy, leased exposure percentages.
We hit on availability, and then we also show you things like applications, cancels, denials, and leases.
How do you get the applications, cancels, denials, and leases data?
Yeah, good question.
So everything here has to do with the fact that we're tracking unit-level data every single day.
And I think the easiest way to explain the transactional data is basically if a unit's available on the property website today, it's going to fall under this available unit bucket.
If tomorrow that unit's gone from the property website, we're going to assume that somebody put in an application on it.
And if that unit doesn't reappear on the website within 7 days, meaning it wasn't canceled, then it becomes a lease.
What's nice about this is.
Smart!
Yeah, what's nice is you can audit this data really easily.
Like, you can drill into all these metrics.
So, first of all, you can see every single unit that's available at a property right now.
If you want to follow the life cycle of a unit, you can see which units are under application.
And then from application, you can see which units leased.
So, always spot on.
I'll tell you, the only caveat would be that, since this is publicly available information, internally, you guys might process an application in 3 days or 4 days.
So, you would just move it over to the lease status a few days quicker than us.
We have that 7-day grace period because, again, it's publicly available information.
Yep.
Yep.
So, that's Section 2.
Section 3 here is just very high-level pricing and availability.
So this is, like, quick and dirty, like, I'm just having a conversation with the client, or internally, I just want to see high level where we stack up.

So, again, inside of here, we'll give you every key metric that you might care about, so you might want to base performance off of rent or price per foot, just sorting this from high to low, low to high, and seeing where you stack up.
We're also going to show you fees and deposits, so just making you aware of the ancillary fees and revenue opportunities out there.
That's nice.
It is, yeah.
Very helpful.
Especially, yeah, it seems like there's some crazy ones out there now, and everyone's trying to….
Obviously, realize a little more revenue.
Yeah, fee transparency, my favorite new topic.

Yep, I live in Phoenix, and I don't know why, but I listen to the local news in the background every morning when I'm having coffee, and that's… that's all they talked about for, like, 3 months straight.
So, there's a ton of new supply here, and I think that's… I don't know, that's a sticking point, so… Yep, but also show you in-unit and community level amenities as well.
Awesome.
Yeah, so this is the market survey.
… I'll show you our floor plan report, and what's nice about this is a lot of our customers like to have this open, you know, for pricing conversations.
If you use a rev management tool, which I think you guys use the, Entrata tool, if I remember correctly.
Yep, yes.
So I know, like, a report here I'll show you in a second, but… A lot of our customers like to have this open if they have a pricing consultant that they work with.
It's just some extra levels of granularity.
But first off, what we do here is we'll just give you the roll-ups, like, we'll show you the average studios or one bedrooms in each property.

So, like, at 805 Leah, studios make up about 20% of that property, their average studio is 508 feet, and then we can give you leased availability and rent trend data over whatever time frame you care about.
What I like, though, is if you flip over here to floor plan, we actually break it down and show you each unique floor plan within a competitive set.
So if we look at, like, the 1x1s, for example, the idea is just that not all 1 bedrooms compete with all other one-bedrooms.
So we can see the smallest one-bed in this comp set's 516 feet at the alcove.
If you scroll all the way to the bottom, the largest one's gonna be over 1,000 feet at, … at the Harlow.
So just making it very easy to compare your ones, or your floor plans off of like or similar size floor plans.
And then, same idea, we can give you unit mix down to this floor plan level, plus that lease availability and rent trend data.
So, I think what's nice, too, is… Yeah, so what's nice, too, is, like, if I toggle on this Canon Music Row property, these guys have, like, 17 different one-bedrooms in the property.
But what's interesting is the A4 floor plan makes up 11% of the property, the A9 makes up 12.
So again, even though there's 17 one-bedrooms, two of them alone make up about a quarter of the entire property.
So you can almost kind of filter out the noise and focus in on what really matters.
And then just a cool feature, like, if you actually click on the floor plan name, we can pull up this modal and give you rent trends, so both gross and net at the floor plan level.
So, green is gross, blue is net, anywhere there's a gap in the data just means they didn't have any A9s available.
And then at the top here, this is where you would toggle between gross and net effective rents.
What else I like, too, is we, we include 5 years' worth of historical data in the application.
So, again, you pick and choose your time frame, you select your interval.
And then you can look at the data at the property level, we're broken out by floor plan.
But inside of here, you'll get your rent trends, so both gross and net rents.

And you can compare, obviously, each individual's property's performance over the given time frame, or if you want, you can toggle over and just compare your property's performance versus the meeting of the comp set.
So you get your rent trends.
We also show you concession history, which, again, can be looked at as percentage or a value.
We'll show you supply.
leasing velocity, And also your occupancy, lease, and exposure percentage trends.
So, seems like post-pandemic, we're back to some, you know, normalization in terms of seasonality, and our customers are really liking the trend reports here, so… pretty beneficial.
But that's the high level of, kind of, the market survey portion of the tool.
I will let you know part two here, research.
This is geared towards, like, new business or acquisitions or new development, but basically what we let you do here is generate what I just showed you for any property in the country.

So if you're looking to underwrite an acquisition, or you're looking to win new business, you just click on New Report, you type in the name or the address of whatever property out there you care about, you find it in the list, you click on it, and 3 seconds later, you have that real-time market survey.
So, basically expose our entire database, anybody.
So this is, like… What if the properties are still under construction?
Yeah, so….
Or, like, plan to be under development.
I have that in Explore here.
So Explore's gonna be more of that co-star view.
So this is market, sub-market data, but you can type in any city, town, neighborhood, zip code, county, you can draw your own boundary.
You can also type in a property address, or a property name, property address, or a point of interest, and get all this market data from a certain radius there.
Okay.
But yeah, so I pulled up Phoenix, that's where I'm based.
… down below here, I can show you details on new supply.
So… Basically there's… if you look at the map, there's two different colored pins.
One's under construction, and one's scheduled construction.
What's cool here, you put your mouse over the pen, we'll give you details on the construction project.
Yeah, can we see what that gives you?
So this is what it gives you right now.
You just get some details on it.
Okay.
Yeah, so this is… yep.

And then over here on the left, what it shows you is how many units and properties make up each stage of construction, as well as when they're slated to be delivered by quarter, plus some details on the size of the project.
Okay.
What else, I guess, what were you looking for in particular?
No, that's good.

Like, we do, like, a pipeline, report that gets generated and sent out, so anytime something is new under construction, or even in the planning phase, we'll kind of ping the owners and be like, hey, if you're looking for management, we'd love to… Exactly.

Or… and we also have a development side, so, like, in some cases, it's like, if it's in an area we're looking to build in, or get into, we could go to them as, like, an investment partner and be like, hey, are you looking for capital, or whatever.
Yeah, makes sense.
Cool.
Yeah, and actually, as part of this research tool, this acquisition part that I was telling you about, what's kind of cool from the development side is we let you create a custom property.

So, from here, if you have, like, that specific plot of land, you can put in the, the cross streets, or if you just say, hey, I want to develop in Scottsdale, Arizona, you can, you know, you can get as granular as you want, and then you can put in… I'm just going to use Scottsdale as an example, just to show you how this works.
then you can put in, you know, whatever year you want to build, or however many units, and then you can actually build out your unit mix.
So if you want to make it simple and just go, hey, I want 1-2s, and threes, or if you want to list 5 different style 1s, 5 different style twos, 5 different style 3s, you can do that.

But you put your average square footage and the rent prices you're looking to achieve, and based off of that, our system will pull in who we consider to be the best comps, and then give you all the same market data.
Justin, now you're speaking my language.
Yeah, so pretty cool.
It's, … Yeah, it's cool.
But what's nice, too, so I'll let you know, whenever we generate a comp set here, these are who our system thinks are the best comps.
But same idea, you can click into here and edit and pick and choose whatever you want to add in.
So… Yeah, so pretty cool.
What about, so understanding the building style, whether it's, like, garden,
Mid-rise, high-rise, are there elevators?
Is there parking?
Yeah.
Yeah, so we can do, …
I'm scrolling down here, we can do… Construction type, so you can get your garden mid-rise, high-rise.
Okay.
And for amenities
Yeah, we have pretty much every option you should see in, in CoStars.
You can see your elevator, you can see.
Okay.
You can see fitness center, you know, you got just about everything.
It just allows us to, like, really granularly dig into, like, where the rents are, because obviously a building with elevators versus a building with stairs is, like, a huge difference.
Oh, yeah.
And then having on-site parking in the city versus not having on-site parking, those are, like, two of the big, like, differentiators when I'm looking at rents, so….
Yep, for sure.
Another thing that I was curious about is what type of, contacts do you have in here?
So, like, we use CoStar to find client… potential client contact information.
It'll tell us who owns the properties.
Yeah, so I can give you an ownership group, I don't have contact information, so what's nice is, like.
you know, I'm in… I just pulled Scottsdale up, but what I'm showing you here is every property in Scottsdale, or, you know, that meets my criteria.
So if I click into Vitri, for example, so first of all, I can show you property, unit, and amenity-specific images.
I can give you ownership information, I can give you ownership history, transactional data, plus management company.
Okay.
I can give you all your building specs.
Perfect.
Yeah, I can… just to show you this really quick, I think it's pretty slick, but we give you, like, a performance trend here, so you can compare the subject versus the comps in the market as a whole.
Okay.
We lay out unit mix really well.
So again, you can see the 1, 2s, and threes if you want, or you can drill into each one and see the additional, you know, floor plans in each.
What else I like is we have the estimated rent roll in here.
So, I think it'd be pretty helpful.
Yes.
And then, if you scroll down, you basically get that market survey view just in this kind of singular view.
So, again, you can compare performance versus the five direct competitors.

The very end of this, we have the transaction history, so you can see cap rate information, you can see… You know, this is just… this is a new property, so obviously not a whole lot of detail here, but we have transaction history going back to 2002 for anything over $2.
5 million.

New supply will be in here as well, so you can kind of pick and choose your radius in regards to what's listed for new supply, but just being aware of, obviously, what's under development and what's planned.
And then you do demographic data from here as well, so 135….

Oh, that was something else I was gonna ask, is, like, when I put together reports for, like, a new development project, I can pull a lot of, like, what's the average household size, what's the… what are the ages of the people in this area, what's the income levels, … employers….
Yep.
So, I have population, education, income, and employment data.

So, I can tell you what's, … unique about ours that I've heard compared to other sources is that rather than giving you the, the demographics at just the market or sub-market level, I can actually take this all the way down to, like, the neighborhood or custom-defined rail.
So, obviously, if you're, like, if you're looking to develop, like, Scottsdale's actually a perfect example.
Like, if I give you the demographics for Scottsdale, it's a, it's a, you know.
predominantly, like, retiree, pre-retiree, you know, demographic, but if you look at Old Town Scottsdale, that data totally flips.
It becomes young, professional, single-family households.
So, I think that the extra level of granularity regarding demographics is pretty valuable from that perspective.
Okay.
What about businesses?
So we use CoStar to run, like.
who are the local businesses?
I can even run a report where I'm like, hey, I want to see all the employers within a mile that have 500-plus employees.
Yeah, we can't do that right now.
That's a data point we're looking into adding.
Okay.
Yeah, not only, like, businesses, but, like, other points of interest, like, you know, is there a Trader Joe's, or a Kroger's, or, you know, what else is in the area?
What's the school district?
Exactly, yeah, so school districts are coming, flood zones are coming, sales comps, land comps are coming.
Forecasting's actually gonna be released here, right after Labor Day.
Okay.

So, I'll tell you, I mean, like, our goal with this is I'm beyond confident that we have the best rent comp data in the industry, but what's interesting is we talk to these companies, we talk to companies all the time, and it's like, everyone kind of uses, like, three, four different data sources together and tries to marry them together.
So, I think what we're trying to build here is, like, literally that one-stop shop for everything multifamily.
So adding in some of those other data points, like you were mentioning, is… What's on our immediate roadmap here?

Yeah, and I mean, the employer data is probably something that we could find elsewhere, but it is something that we use, like, in our lease-up plans, like, it helps us just understand who are we going to focus outreach on, where do we think our residents are going to be, like, coming from.
Makes sense.
So that would be helpful.
That's really the only thing, honestly, when I'm looking at this, that I don't see that we use pretty regularly, but I feel like I could play around with some of this other data and fill it in.
Yeah, like, right here, like, I'm guessing you'd want, like, names of actual businesses or companies, right?
Like, I could show you how many employees are in each segment, but not necessarily that they work for… Amazon, or work for… Right.
Like, in Baltimore, if we can show a client that they're within one mile of Johns Hopkins Hospital, they're like, this is great, let's build it.
Makes sense.
Cool.
Yeah, and what's interesting about that is, I think, I mean.
like, I mean, the Johns Hopkins, for example, I mean, that's an easy one to map out.
Maybe some of the other small, like, the smaller businesses would be tough, but, I mean, that's an easy one, I think, just to add in from, like, a Google Maps overlay or something.

Yeah, and I mean, I think the only reason they have that data is because they're not just multifamily, like, they… it's commercial real estate and all that other stuff, so it's probably easy for them to just feed that over, but… For sure.
I'll say.

Honestly… The thing that I like the most about this is that we would manually pull data out of CoStar to put into a market survey, and the market survey layout that you guys have, one, is very similar to what we would be filling out, and two.
they wouldn't then have to fill it out.
Exactly, yeah.

Yep, and just so you're aware, too, I didn't show you this, but that export I was talking about, just know that it comes through looking pretty, pretty sharp, so this is what that looks like into Excel, so a nice, clean, professional look to it.
I also really like the, unlimited access.

I think it would be cool to give owners, like, restricted access to, like, based on their port, like, properties, like, they can pull a report, because they ask us for that stuff all the time, and it's… I think that they would like having that access, and it would kind of be, like, a value add.
For sure.
Yeah, and along those lines, what's nice is we offer tons of training, so we can do group sessions, one-on-one sessions, ongoing training sessions as you get new employees.
So whoever you set up, we do a nice job of making sure they're comfortable navigating, accessing the platform, and getting the data that they're looking for.

We'd also love to, probably, if we go down this road, get, like, a recorded one done, and we can load it into Grace Hill, and make it, like, part of the training onboarding for every associate, and then if they need, like, that backup, like, maybe we could do something, like, quarterly with you guys.
Sure, yeah, we actually have it… we actually have one baked into the application, but we could get an external link of it.
Like, in this Help Center here, you can always click in, and there's… there's modules and everything, like, terms and… or terms and stuff.
So, yeah, absolutely, we can get you all that stuff on… in there.

Okay, and then I think for me, like, I would really want, like, maybe a deeper session on, like, the stuff that's specific to, like, the new development, and just making sure that I know what to pull, … But even, like I said, the market survey that you use is really similar to the, like, the rent analysis that I do.
So I… I like this, so I'd like to go back to Melissa and have a discussion with her, and just make sure that we're all on the same page and that I didn't miss anything.
Yeah, makes sense.
Perfect.
I will… I'll plan to follow up with Melissa.
Today's Wednesday.
I'll just reach out to her on maybe Friday or beginning of next week, just to see how your internal conversations are going.
If any questions come up while you're talking to her, or after we get off the call, you think, oh shoot, I forgot to ask that, feel free to reach out.
I'll send you a quick recap email of what we discussed.
I'll include the sample reports, just so you have something to reference.
But yeah, I look forward to continuing the conversation.
I did have one question that I wrote down.
I know you guys said that you aggregate the data from the property websites directly, right?
Yep.
Who and what does that?
And how often?
Yeah, so it's done every single night, and we.
Okay.
I think a scraper is probably the easiest way to explain it, but basically a web crawler that goes out there.
Okay.

And then, I'll let you know, too, like, in regards to concessions, we use, like, a large language model, like AI, to go out there and collect concessions, parse them out, apply them to peripheral floor plans, units, give you the text or, like, the detail of the concession.
So, a scraper slash AI is kind of the….
No, that's what I was hoping, because I was like, if it's, like, people, like.
Who knows if they're, you know….
Okay, it's me.
Yeah, that's Jamie, just scanning property websites.
I'm not concerned, Jamie.
I think you'd be good at it, but…
Okay, great.
Cool.
Well, we appreciate your time, I'm glad that we were able to catch up with you.
If anything comes up, let me know, but I will send a recap email, and then I'll plan to touch base with Melissa end of week or early next.
Perfect.
Thank you so much.
Awesome.
Thanks, Becky.
Have a good one, guys.
You too.
Bye.
